item.title: [FHIR-55267] NutritionOrder.intent has no escape valve
item.description:
The element NutritionOrder.intent is a mandatory element with a required binding, but appears to have neither an 'unknown' or 'other' concept, which can make the containing element impossible to populate in some cases. Please add 'unknown', 'other', or some equivalent escape valve concept to the value set.

(Comment 231 - imported by: Lloyd McKenzie)
item.summary: NutritionOrder.intent has no escape valve

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

comments.comment: At 2026-02-12 OO main call it decided that as the valueset and codesystem do not belong to OO we should complete our review as with other tickets, note our recommendation, and then re-assign the ticket to the appropriate work group. In this case the valueset is owned by FHIR-I and the codesystem by CDS. Consensus was to send these to FHIR-I if any change is recommended.

Similar to https://jira.hl7.org/browse/FHIR-55275

This is bound to a code system that is not owned by OO and spans multiple resources: https://build.fhir.org/codesystem-request-intent.html.

The code system does not support the other/unknown escape valve concept.

Further work would be required here.

item.title: [FHIR-55842] Clarify "at minimum" return for primary hooks
item.description:
In the Hook Categories section, it states "CRD Servers SHALL, at minimum, return a Coverage Information system action for 'primary' hooks, even if the response indicates that further information is needed or that the level of detail provided is insufficient to determine coverage. §hook-16"

Does "at minimum" mean a Coverage Information system action must always be present in the response, or only if no other cards would be return. I think it could be interpreted either way.

Assuming that the intention is that a Coverage Information system action must always be returned, suggest updating the sentence to something like,

"CRD Servers SHALL include a Coverage Information system action in the response for 'primary' hooks, even if the response indicates that further information is needed or that the level of detail provided is insufficient to determine coverage. §hook-16"
item.summary: Clarify "at minimum" return for primary hooks

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: Git master 355fc42
Reviewed and accepted proposed disposition on Feb. 19 BR call
The individual sections for the primary hook types contain similar language. Recommend that they be updated in a similar way.

The description of the coverage information response type on the response profiles page provides additional detail on a case where a coverage information response might not be provided, namely when there is already a coverage-information extension on the submitted request resource and no updates are needed.

Based on that, I would be ok with keeping the "at minimum" language as long as a link to the response type section were added, e.g. the following or similar:

"CRD Servers SHALL, at minimum, return a Coverage Information system action for 'primary' hooks, even if the response indicates that further information is needed or that the level of detail provided is insufficient to determine coverage. §hook-16 See the Coverage Information Response Type section of this guide for additional details on returning coverage information including when it is acceptable for CRD servers to omit this response type on primary hooks."

item.title: [FHIR-55849] Additional Prefetch Capabilities should reference CDS Hooks
item.description:
The Additional Prefetch Capabilities section has very similar content to a section in the Jan 2026 ballot of the CDS Hook spec. The CRD section states

"A proposal has been submitted suggesting how to address this issue. The work group responsible for the specification has proposed adopting a modified version of this proposal that does not include _include support. This version of the implementation guide pre-adopts that proposal."

Is the content in the CDS Hooks spec the pre-adopted proposal and can it be referenced instead of duplicating it in CRD, even though it is not an official publication?
item.summary: Additional Prefetch Capabilities should reference CDS Hooks

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

item.title: [FHIR-55908] safeguards for "DTR launch" to not become a default
item.description:
My main concern is the potential for payers to default to “launch DTR” whenever information is incomplete, or their system cannot process a request in time. Basically, pushing the provider downstream. They are technically conformant, but it doesn't help determine the coverage. So like, "launch DTR" could become the fallback response whenever a payer hasn’t completed prefetching or doesn't have all internal logic figured out, and it doesn't count as a error.

Can we add verbiage that would help mitigate the loophole? This may also imapact the conformance language section
item.summary: safeguards for "DTR launch" to not become a default

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: Git master 996a394
Reviewed and accepted proposed disposition on Feb. 20 BR call

celine_lefebvre I'm not sure what conformance language we could write that would be testable/enforceable.  Because there are so many variations around how data might be coded, where it might be exposed, what might be needed, response times for query from EMRs, granularity at which orders are captured, etc. it's super-difficult to close the window in a way that still allows for the real-world reality that there are lots of legitimate situations where DTR will be needed.

If you have proposed wording, we could consider it.  However, I think this particular loophole is best closed via metrics.  If certain payers have really poor response rates compared with their peers, CMS or others could opt to impose consequences.  That removes the need to set specific rules and instead focuses on outcomes - which is what we really care about anyhow.

item.title: [FHIR-55771] Coverage prefetch example is awkward
item.description:
The coverage prefetch example in the prefetch section could be improved in several ways:

  1. It is not clear why this is needed at this point in the narrative. The previous paragraph is a discussion of how only a single Coverage will be returned in the prefetch key, it doesn't feel to me like it requires an example and the next paragraph around the minimum set of prefetch data that Clients need to support seems more critical to this section and I think should at minimum go above this example (maybe the minimum should go above the whole discussion of coverages).
  2. It contains non-coverage prefetch keys, including `encounter` and `device` (empty). Suggest removing those other prefetch keys from the example. 
  3. It is a long example (even with removing additional keys) that interrupts the reading flow. Suggest making it collapsable like it done in some US Core example such as in this section.
item.summary: Coverage prefetch example is awkward

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: Git master 586c5a9
Reviewed and accepted proposed disposition on Feb. 19 BR call

item.title: [FHIR-55782] Define more clearly the minimum level of prefetch support for clients
item.description:
The IG currently is clear on the requirement to support prefetch, but not on what the minimum level of support it, leaving conformance testing tools to fill in the gaps.

Things that are clear:

  • Clients must support prefetch: "For this release of the IG, conformant CRD clients and servers SHALL support the CDS Hooks prefetch capability. §found-22"
  • Servers get to define the prefetches they need: "Each CRD server will define the prefetch requests for their CRD server based on the information they require to provide coverage requirements. They MAY include more and/or less prefetch requests than described in this Additional Data Retrieval section, based on which data is desired. §found-25"

What is not clear is client responsibility around providing requested information via prefetch

  • "Clients SHALL be able to supply all the existing resources defined in the prefetch section below for request resources they support. §found-23 " - since this is in the general "additional data retrieval" section, presumably clients have the option to meet this requirement either via prefetch or by supporting queries for the data. However, the link to the "prefetch section below" muddies the waters.
  • "The minimum set of prefetch expectations for all hook types that must be supported by all CRD clients ... [discovery response]" - this statement in the referenced section does not provide a precise definition of either the resources in scope or what it means to meet these expectations. Readers are left to infer the resource types based on the queries and are not told whether they are allowed to defer any or all of these to retrieval by the payer via FHIR API.

Request that this section be clarified to include:

  1. An explicit list of resource types that clients SHALL be able to provide as prefetched data within hook requests. For example, this could be done by adding a conformance verb to "The base requirement for these prefetches, whether based on Encounter or one of the request resources, is to bring back the following associated resources:" and adding explicit resource names / links to the list that follows.
  2. A clear description of what queries they need to support via prefetch. Looking at the example discovery response, it seems like it is patient and encounter reads, coverage search by patient + status, and search by _id (including multiple values) for all other types. Are clients required to be able to provide the requested data via prefetch when payers request it that way, or are they allowed to decide not support providing it via prefetch?
item.summary: Define more clearly the minimum level of prefetch support for clients

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: Git master 586c5a9
Reviewed and accepted proposed disposition on Feb. 19 BR call

item.title: [FHIR-55917] Correct Types for dispatchedOrders and fulfillmentTasks in CRD Order Dispatch Context
item.description:
Current Behavior:

  • On the CDS Hooks Specific Context for Order-Dispatch logical definition, both dispatchedOrders and fulfillmentTasks are currently defined as Bundles.
  • As confirmed with Lloyd McKenzie, this is incorrect and requires correction.

Proposed Correction:

  • dispatchedOrders should be a list of FHIR local references.
  • fulfillmentTasks should be a list of Task objects.
item.summary: Correct Types for dispatchedOrders and fulfillmentTasks in CRD Order Dispatch Context

Reviewed and accepted proposed disposition on Feb. 19 BR call

item.title: [FHIR-55774] Unclear what "minimum set of prefetch expectations" mean
item.description:
"The minimum set of prefetch expectations for all hook types that must be supported by all CRD clients and solicited (when relevant) by all CRD servers is as follows:

[... example discovery response with requested prefetches for each hook ...]

Other information will need to be retrieved using queries that are more specific to the type of hook being invoked and the resources passed with it."

Specific clarifications and adjustments that would be helpful:

  1. Is there a conformance requirement for clients to support these specific prefetch queries? The use of "must" suggests that maybe they are, but no conformance verb is actually used. I suspect the rub is that payers will adjust these in practice, so requiring support for specific prefetch queries is potentially overkill for clients if payers don't use them. I wonder if framing these as examples of prefetch queries that would cover the entire scope of the minimum data described in the parent section would be a clearer way to convey how these operate in the guide: a potential implementation approach for payers that clients can use as a starting point to check their level of support?
  2. "Other information will need to be retrieved using queries that are more specific to the type of hook being invoked and the resources passed with it.": I find this sentence very confusing in this context:
    1. Payers are constructing these prefetch queries to request specific data, but clients are executing those queries when providing it via prefetch. So "retrieved" seems to me to be the wrong word for this sentence because it is about what payers will put in their discovery responses around prefetch. So I think it should be "requested" instead of "retrieved".
    2. I think this sentence is intended to be a re-iteration of the possibility that payers can request additional data that goes beyond the minimum data described in the parent section would and a comment about how payers will need to go about constructing the prefetch specification (by looking at the hook specifications), but I'm filling in a lot of gaps. Recommend that the sentence be cleaned up and clarified.
  3. The specific discovery response is long and breaks up the narrative flow. Three things I think would help.
    1. Move any related text above the example so that it doesn't get missed
    2. break out the example into chunks for each hook so that links can be created for each
    3. Make part or all collapsable like in this section of us core so that readers can get the information when needed without interrupting the narrative flow
item.summary: Unclear what "minimum set of prefetch expectations" mean

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: Git master 586c5a9
Reviewed and accepted proposed disposition on Feb. 19 BR call

item.title: [FHIR-52061] Support code modifiers beyond CPT
item.description:
The CRD guide says:

If using CPT, note the ability to convey CPT modifier codes via post-coordination as described in the Using CPT page on terminology.hl7.org.

This is error prone (implementations that don't expect modifiers will not understand the code), limiting (clients are unable to send modifiers for HCPCS codes), and not very idiomatic FHIR (this convention originates from X12). CRD should introduce an extension to carry the modifier instead of relying on this syntax.
item.summary: Support code modifiers beyond CPT

status.description: There is agreement that a change of some sort needs to be made in response to the requested change

comments.comment: Reviewed and approved proposed disposition on Oct. 15 BR call;;;
Good catch.  That actually makes it easier for us, as we'd just specify the modifier codes directly with no need for code + value.;;;
Just making a note here that orderDetail is a CodeableConcept in FHIR R4, and orderDetail.parameter.code and value are R5+;;;

Information from the AHA and AMA:HCPCS is divided into 2 main subsystems — Level I and Level II.HCPCS Level II codes are distinct from Current Procedural Terminology (CPT®) codes (Level I), which are numeric and maintained by the American Medical Association (AMA). * CPT® is a uniform coding system consisting of descriptive terms and identifying codes used primarily to identify medical services and procedures furnished by physicians and other health care professionals for which they bill public or private health insurance programs. * CPT® codes consist of 5 numeric digits.CPT (Current Procedural Terminology) code modifiers are two-character codes added to a five-digit CPT code to provide additional information about a service or procedure. ** They clarify circumstances that affect the service, such as whether it was bilateral, when reporting a separate and distinct service, more extensive than usual, or involved multiple surgeons. Using modifiers correctly is critical for proper billing and reimbursement. The AMA maintains and holds copyright for CPT Modifiers. For example, Modifier 59 is used to identify procedures/services, other than E/M services, that are not normally reported together, but are appropriate under the circumstances, i.e.,  different session, different procedure or surgery, different site or organ system, separate incision/excision, separate lesion, or separate injury (or area of injury in extensive injuries) not ordinarily encountered or performed on the same day by the same individual. * HCPCS Level II is a standardized coding system that is used primarily to identify products, supplies, and services not included in the CPT® codes, such as ambulance services or durable medical equipment, prosthetics, orthotics, and supplies (DMEPOS) when used outside a physician's office. *  These are used by Medicare and other insurers to cover these services that aren't specifically identified by CPT® codes. * HCPCS Level II codes (also known as alpha-numeric codes) consist of a single alphabetical letter followed by 4 numeric digits.HCPCS code modifiers are established internally by CMS to facilitate accurate Medicare claims processing. Modifiers are assigned for use when the information provided by a HCPCS code descriptor needs to be supplemented to identify specific circumstances that may apply to an item or service. For example, the UE modifier is used when the item identified by a HCPCS code is "used equipment," and the NU modifier is used for "new equipment." The HCPCS Level II modifiers are either alpha-numeric or two letters.Please reference the document ”HEALTHCARE COMMON PROCEDURE CODING SYSTEM (HCPCS) LEVEL II CODING PROCEDURES” for additional detail regarding HCPCS Level II codes and background.;;;

apreisler will check on whether there are documented conventions for post-coordination of HCPCS codes.  November/Cooper will see how they handle modifiers (or question/answers that get mapped to them) in Epic.  Dan will check Meditech.;;;

Part of the issue is that other code systems (e.g. SNOMED) also have modifiers and those definitely mustn't be sent using extensions.  Where post-coordination is allowed, terminology best practice is to send the code as a single element.  (It's less verbose and lets simple systems handle post-coordinated concepts as if they were a single standard code.)  For CPT, at least, the AMA is saying that post-coordination is an official thing.  We'll need to figure out what to do with HCPCS.;;;

lloyd As far as I can tell, HCPCS defines modifiers, but not a specific syntax for their use, and the HL7 terminology guide has no guidance on their use either. X12 (for claims or for prior authorization) uses discrete fields to distinguish between codes and modifiers, regardless of the code system, but the reason the HL7 terminology guide seems to have adopted colon separation is that the separator between those discrete fields is typically (but not always) a colon. If FHIR specs were to reflect the spirit of X12, they would have a separate element for code modifiers, as is done currently in PAS.;;;

The use of modifiers is part of the CPT code syntax.  It's officially described on the HL7 terminology page for CPT.  AMA's working on an update, but that won't change that post-coordination is allowed.  Given that there's a 'standard' way to communicate modifiers, introducing an extension would be inappropriate.Does HCPCS also define a modifier syntax?  What's done in X12 claims now?  We should expect FHIR to follow the same convention.;;;

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: We potentially have authorization tokens in three places: * In the CRD request, an auth token is provided to support subsequent EHR access by the CRD service.  There are examples of this already.  This is present in the CRD call, not the response. * This auth token is subsequently used in the HTTP header when making query calls against the EHR.  Because it's in the authorization header, there's no good way to show it in examples. * Auth tokens can also come into play when using either SMART back-end authentication or UDAP authentication, but the examples for how to do that are covered by their respective specifications.

item.title: [FHIR-55761] Confusing use of "must"
item.description: "Where a CRD client opts to not block users from proceeding for responses that come back in a period of time longer than the target time window in this guide (i.e., 5s or 10s), the client must ensure that users are made aware of the information when it is available."Is the use of "must" instead of "SHALL" an explicit decision not to make this a conformance requirement? It is otherwise phrased like a conformance requirement and it would be natural for it to be one (it is a feature that the system could implement). Recommend that this use a conformance verb, either SHALL if it is a hard requirement or SHOULD if it is a strong recommendation.
item.summary: Confusing use of "must"

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: Git master bdad79d
Reviewed and accepted proposed disposition on Feb 18 BR call;;;
Note that there are two typos in this next sentence (words highlighted in red in the previous comment);;;

After looking more closely at the next sentence ("For responses that come back in a time period that exceeds the time maximm duration specifd in this guide, CRD clients MAY ignore the resulting cards and/or system actions"), I think that the previous sentence should be a SHOULD or simply be removed, since clients MAY ignore these results (meaning the user wouldn't be notified.;;;

item.title: [FHIR-55764] Conflicting Additional Data Retrieval Requirements
item.description: I think that the following two narrative requirements around requested prefetch data are in conflict and cannot both be met: # Prefetch requests SHOULD only include information that is always expected to be needed for each hook invocation. §found-26 # When information is only needed for certain invocations of the hook (e.g., for specific types of medications or services), that information SHALL only be retrieved by query using the provided token, never requested universally via prefetch. §found-27If a payer decides to request via prefetch a piece of information that is not always needed as they are allowed to do by the first requirement, then they are not following the second requirement.
item.summary: Conflicting Additional Data Retrieval Requirements

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: Git master 1117148
Reviewed and accepted proposed disposition on Feb 18 BR call;;;
We need to make them both SHOULD or both SHALL.  At this point in the process, I'm inclined to go with SHOULD as this will be difficult to evaluate as SHALL.;;;

item.title: [FHIR-55799] Member/coverage matching failures should not use not-covered
item.description:
Currently the requirement for CRD servers when member or coverage matching failed is to indicate "not-covered", which is defined as:

No coverage or possibility of coverage for this service

This doesn't accurately describe what the server is indicating. The server cannot know if there is coverage or possibility of coverage for this service, because the server does not know what coverage rules to apply. This causes problems both because payers do not want to give guidance where they do not know what guidance is appropriate, and clients may render the guidance as "Not covered" (there should be extra information present, but this is a confusing description of the information provided by the payer).This could be alleviated by requiring the use of "conditional" instead, though that doesn't quite match the other uses of "conditional".I would be inclined to add a new covered value of "unknown". This would apply when the CRD server cannot evaluate any rules for reasons other than missing information that the client might produce in-workflow. For example: * This case where there is not (necessarily) missing information, but incorrect information about the member demographics and coverage details, which we would not expect to be resolved at a later stage in the lifecycle of the order * Another system that is required to evaluate coverage rules is unavailable or returned an error * The member indicated to the payer that they do not want their information transmitted over this service * Probably other cases I haven't thought ofSome of these cases could be handled with an OperationOutcome instead, but in a delegated scenario, the CRD endpoint may have some requested services that succeeded and some that failed. In any case, I think it's valuable to have a mechanism to provide additional information about what happened to produce that error in the same structure as a successful CRD response.
item.summary: Member/coverage matching failures should not use not-covered

Will add "Indeterminate" as a new coverage code with the definition:"Indicates that, due to technical issues on the part of the client and/or server, consent restrictions by the patient, or similar atypical limitations or constraints, the payer is unable to evaluate coverage."2. item.title: [FHIR-55805] List of CDS customizations out of sync with page content
item.description: In the intro to the deviations page there is the following list of customizations:"This implementation guide extends/customizes CDS Hooks in 5 ways: additional hook resources, a hook configuration mechanism, additional prefetch capabilities, additional response capabilities, and the ability to link hooks to their corresponding request."This list doesn't line up with the sub-sections of the page. Recommend they be synced up with changes to each by organizing sub-sections as follows: * CRD Version declaration in 11.1 * New hook configuration mechanism in 11.2, with sub sections ** Configuration options extension in 11.2.1 (currently 11.3) ** Hook configuration extension in 11.2.2 (currently 11.3.1) * Additional prefetch capabilities in 11.3 (currently 11.4) * Additional response capabilities in 11.4 (currently 11.5) * Linking cards to requests in 11.5 (currently 11.6) * Controlling hook invocation in 11.6 (currently 11.7)And the following updated narrative list (or similar, or leave it out so it doesn't have to adjust with the content):"This implementation guide extends/customizes CDS Hooks in 5 ways: a CRD version declaration mechanism, a hook configuration mechanism, additional prefetch capabilities, additional response capabilities, the ability to link hooks to their corresponding request, and other controls on hook invocation."
item.summary: List of CDS customizations out of sync with page content

item.title: [FHIR-55966] Exclusive bounds for Observation.referenceRange
item.description:
The topic regarding exclusive bounds for reference ranges has already been discussed on a few occasions, see

  • Jira-Ticket: https://jira.hl7.org/browse/FHIR-51566 
  • Zulip: https://chat.fhir.org/#narrow/channel/179166-implementers/topic/Exclusive.20Observation.2EreferenceRange/with/290861524

As a result of these discussions, the datatype for Observation.referenceRange.low and Observation.referenceRange.high has been changed from SimpleQuantity to Quantity in R6. This change allow to explicitly add a comparator to each of the bounds, which has been something that laboratory experts as well as lab systems in Germany have been strongly advocating for.

However, as we are still using R4 in Germany as well as in the HL7 Europe specifications, a solution for the transition time is needed. A few possible approaches are outlined below:

  • A frequently mentioned solution is the usage of a certain number of decimal places - i.e. x.999999 or x.000001. Theoretically, this is a possible workaround, but lab system vendors mentioned that such a logic would result in a huge maintenance workload due to frequent updates of references ranges. Therefore, this was not seen as a preferable solution for the lab community.
  • As soon as the cross-version extensions are officially published the respective extension for the exclusive bounds could be utilised. As this will probably still take a bit of time, we have to consider to provide such extensions for the HL7 Europe lab report in advance. Having a clear and unified solution on the European level would certainly be a benefit for the cross-border data exchange.
  • A solution with minimal effort, or a fallback solution for systems that cannot handle the extension, would be to ensure that the Observation.referenceRange.text element is always populated. This would ensure that no information is lost.

This topic needs to be discussed in-depth and a solution needs to be agreed on that considers both cross-border and national exchange of lab data.
item.summary: Exclusive bounds for Observation.referenceRange

item.title: [FHIR-55968] traceability
item.description:
I understand the current goal of the IG is to be purely a transport and thus there is no requirements on the FHIR Document content. However, this leaves some problems. These problems can be addressed as I will describe independent of the IG, but I put this comment in as a chance to get an Option or recommendation be added so that the problems are at least understood, better that there is consistent recommendations, or best that there is a named option for product declaration.

When a recipient downloads the FHIR Document, they may further use the data in ways that were not part of the intent. However these downstream activities will not be detectable as to where that content came from. 

Add a recommendation for a unique ID per share :

  • You can distinguish one sharing event from another.
  • You can detect re-sharing by the recipient.
  • You can correlate a disclosure to a consent.
  • You can reconstruct a chain of custody.

Add a Provenance inside the FHIR Document Bundle, where the Provenance can explicitly express a few more details of the sharing. Specifically when the share request was initiated (not when it was read), unique share request identifier, who requested the share (given authorized agents are allowed beyond the Patient), and possibly a why it was shared.  I understand adding this detail may be a step too far, and a Provenance is rather easy for a malicious actor to remove. I point it out anyway for completeness. Identifying it to your readers as a possibility may be useful.

I would be glad to further provide details and examples.
item.summary: traceability

item.title: [FHIR-54825] Consider _maxresults as well
item.description:
valid prefetch templates MUST only make use of:

  • instance level read interactions (for resources with known ids such as Patient, Practitioner, or Encounter)
  • type level search interactions; e.g. {{patient={
    Unknown macro: {context.patientId}

    }}}

  • Resource references (e.g. patient={{context.patientId{}}})
  • token search parameters using equality (e.g. code=4548-4) and optionally the :in modifier (no other modifiers for token parameters)
  • date search parameters on date, dateTime, instant, or Period types only, and using only the prefixes eq, lt, gt, ge, le
  • the _count parameter to limit the number of results returned
  • the sort parameter to allow for _most recent and first queries

This list should include _maxresults as well as this is intended to signal the number of resources that should be returned while _count only lists the size of the Bundle.
item.summary: Consider _maxresults as well

item.title: [FHIR-55904] §resp-28 conformance issue
item.description: ||§resp-28|SHALL|X|CRD Client|exchange|If a CRD client submits a claim related to an order for which it has received a coverage-information extension for the coverage type associated with the claim, that claim SHALL include the coverage-assertion-id and, if applicable, the satisfied-pa-id in the X12 837 K3 segment.|As satisfied-pa-id is defined as follows:|Extension.extension:satisfied-pa-id||Slice Name|satisfied-pa-id||Definition|An identifier indicating that prior authorization requirements have been met||Short|Satisfied prior auth number||Comments|When operating under the CMS enforcement discretion, this element is a prior authorization number and can be submitted in the corresponding X12 element when submitting an eventual claim| Feedback: A prior authorization number is not sent in the K3 segment of the 837. There are specific REF segments to convey the Prior Authorization Number, i.e. REF - PRIOR AUTHORIZATION. The K3 may only be used when approved by X12 and only when it is for data not already accommodated in the TR3.
item.summary: §resp-28 conformance issue

item.title: [FHIR-55800] "What if" support requires non-existant configuration option support
item.description: In the What-if Hook Invocation section:"In the specific case of order-based hooks, "what if" SHOULD use the Order Sign hook but SHALL use the configuration option that prevents the return of an unsolicited determination and MAY use configuration options to prevent the return of other irrelevant types of cards (e.g., duplicate therapy). §found-35"This sentence * references a configuration option that "prevents the return of an unsolicited determination" without linking to it. Reviewing the Configuration options extension section, I don't see any definition of that extension. * references configuration options for blocking certain card types, which is documented in that section and has an explicit requirement ( §dev-5) that could be linked to.Request that the sentence be updated with appropriate links to the target options and also make clear that client support for configuration options is required to support "What if" queries since it is otherwise optional.
item.summary: "What if" support requires non-existant configuration option support

item.title: [FHIR-55399] Add ICD-11 Support for Condition and BodyStructure
item.description:
During the WGM in Cologne the Issue of supporting ICD-11 was risen. 

This is some guidance on where and how ICD-11 can be used (from Linn B.):

When adding a reference to a ICD-11 value set:  #
Use the ID: http://id.who.int/icd/release/11/mms (taken from https://terminology.hl7.org/7.0.0/external_code_systems.html and  International Classification of Diseases, 11th Revision Mortality and Morbidity Statistics (MMS) - HL7 Terminology (THO) v7.0.0)  


  1. I expect us to need a value set for 

  • Condition.code (The main condition/stem code).  (most important)

  • Condition.bodySite (second most important)

  • BodySite.laterality (second most important)

  • BodySite.morphology (I'll figure these out once I get feedback on the first ones)

  • BodySite.location  (I'll figure these out once I get feedback on the first ones)

  • BodySite.locationQualifyer  (I'll figure these out once I get feedback on the first ones)

 
These are my suggestion for logical definition.  *
For value set for condition.code: Include all codes in chapters 1-26 from http://id.who.int/icd/release/11/mms 
 


  • For value set for condition.bodySite:  Include codes from http://id.who.int/icd/release/11/mms where concepts descends from Anatomy and topography http://id.who.int/icd/entity/1154280071

  *
For value set for BodySite.laterality:  Include codes from http://id.who.int/icd/release/11/mms where concepts descends from Laterality http://id.who.int/icd/entity/1435602785

 
(For later
For value set for BodySite.morphology:  Include codes from http://id.who.int/icd/release/11/mms where concepts descends from (later)
 
For value set for BodySite.location:  Include codes from http://id.who.int/icd/release/11/mms where concepts descends from (later)
 
For value set for BodySite.locationQualifyer:  Include codes from http://id.who.int/icd/release/11/mms where concepts descends from (later)
item.summary: Add ICD-11 Support for Condition and BodyStructure

item.description:
The slice diagnostic-service on DiagnosticReport is required. I agree that a predictable value on this field is important. The choice for Valueset-diagnostic-service-sections - FHIR v5.0.0 is unfortunate. The valueset was designed as an example and not intended to be required.

Please use a different valueset or make it extensible.

item.title: [FHIR-55508] Add upper limit to Address.line cardinality
item.description:
Excessively long Addresses can cause all kinds of practical issues with label printers, windowed envelopes etc.

Having an upper limit would help to make sure that Adresses can be printed reliably.

We have limited the cardinality to  0..3 in the German base profiles in keeping with DIN 5008 norm for addresses.

item.title: [FHIR-55526] Inconsistent cardinality for attester elements
item.description:
Composition.attester.mode and .time are cardinality 1..1, with .time profiled from the base Composition - but .party still has cardinality 0..1, despite the Xt-EHR logical model listing .attester as 1..1.

Should this be changed? Or, what is the reason the cardinality differs from the base FHIR resource and the logical model?
item.summary: Inconsistent cardinality for attester elements

comments.comment: For flexibility reasons I'd prefer to keep party 0.. , to allow indicating that a document has been attested withouth necessarly saying who did it; but I agree that in this case we should align with the model.

item.title: [FHIR-55513] Use of “imposeProfile” in Practitioner / PractitionerRole / Organization
item.description:
The current profiles use the structuredefinition‑imposeProfile extension to reference the IPS profiles, but no version is specified. As a result, fixed versions of the EU‑Core/Base profiles (e.g., 1.0.0) implicitly refer to the latest IPS version. Changes in the IPS profiles therefore retroactively affect older EU‑Core versions, leading to non‑transparent dependencies, potential conflicts, and additional effort for implementers.

Recommendation: Canonical URLs within the impose mechanism should include a fixed version to clearly bind the EU‑Core profiles to a specific IPS release. For example: "valueCanonical" : "http://hl7.org/fhir/uv/ips/StructureDefinition/Practitioner-uv-ips|2.0.0"

Alternatively, the specification documentation should at least clearly state which IPS version the profiles conceptually rely on.
item.summary: Use of “imposeProfile” in Practitioner / PractitionerRole / Organization

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: impose should be removed completly, since EU CORE/Base should be the one and only source of truth

base profiles seem not to have imposeProfile

I agree that for core imposeProfile is not correct (we are not adopting IPS obligations for the core)

Evaluate to remove the  imposeProfile or covert it into a SDcompliesWithProfile extensions (experimental extension ?)

base profiles seem not to have imposeProfile

I agree that for core imposeProfile is not correct (we are not adopting IPS obligations for the core)

Evaluate to remove the  imposeProfile or covert it into a SDcompliesWithProfile extensions (experimental extension ?)

item.title: [FHIR-55543] Harmonization of Slice Names across EU IGs
item.description:
Please harmonize Slice names for extensions across european IGs. E.g. the extension for the diagnosticReport-reference from the composition is called "diagnosticReport" here, but "diagnosticReport-reference" in the HL7 Europe Laboratory Report IG. (https://build.fhir.org/ig/hl7-eu/laboratory/en/StructureDefinition-Composition-eu-lab.html)

Please ensure a centralized check for coherent naming.
item.summary: Harmonization of Slice Names across EU IGs

status.description: Decision has been reached that no change will be made to the specification based on the requested change

comments.comment: Maybe a public clear naming convention would make sense.

If it is harmonized with inheritance, that is also fine.

We would suggest to keep the slice names consistent, so if you use slicenames with "-reference" suffix in the majority of other places (e.g. composition-reference and alike), than it would make sense to use if consistently. Otherwise diagnosticReport is fine, if consistent with other sliceNames.

When the Composition core will be published the other guides will dervie from this one, so all the slice names will be harmonized across the guides.

Are you suggesting to call this slice diagnosticReport-reference ?

item.title: [FHIR-55424] MedicalTestResultEUCore unclear .category requirements
item.description:
MedicalTestResultEUCore profile contains two slices on `.category`. One for code laboratory and one for vital-signs.

The mapping page (https://hl7.eu/fhir/base/2.0.0-ballot/map-ehdsobservation.html) does not list the reason for using these two codes only from the longer list of codes in FHIR Core.

For example procedure presumably could yield medical test results too. Profiling just the current two category codes signals that these are the only relevant categories? 

Propose to extend the mapping table and/or profile to make clear how to interpret the applied slicing, or to remove the slicing and leave the FHIR Core specification as-is

item.title: [FHIR-55955] 'item' has duplicates slices for 'questionnaire-hidden' extension
item.description:
In the 2.2.0-snapshot build, the DTR Base Questionnaire profile inherits from SDC Base Questionnaire in the 4.0.0-snapshot (https://build.fhir.org/ig/HL7/sdc/en/StructureDefinition-sdc-questionnaire.html); this base profile already defines a Must Support 0..1 slice on `item` for the questionnaire-hidden extension (http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/questionnaire-hidden). The 2.2.0-snapshot DTR Base Qnr profile also defines an (MS, 0..1)  slice named `itemHidden` for this same extension.

I don't believe this will cause any validation issues but it is a little confusing.
item.summary: 'item' has duplicates slices for 'questionnaire-hidden' extension

item.title: [FHIR-55790] No-active-coverage is too specific
item.description:
The description of the no-active-coverage coverage information reason currently reads:

The referenced insurance coverage for the member is not active, so no coverage information can be provided

For context, the no-member-found reason reads:

The CRD server was unable to find a matching member, so no coverage information can be provided

With these two options, there is no ability to indicate that a coverage could not be found, only that the member could not be found or that the coverage found as was inactive. Without the description, I would assume "no-active-coverage" would include the scenario where an active coverage is not found, but "the [..] coverage [..] is not active" indicates that the coverage was found.I would suggest editing the description of "no-active-coverage" to say something like:

The CRD server was unable to find a unique active coverage matching the information in the request, so no coverage information can be provided.
item.summary: No-active-coverage is too specific

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: Git: master 362cb40Still need to complete the UTG proposal;;;
Reviewed and accepted proposed disposition on Feb. 13 BR call;;;

item.title: [FHIR-50276] Migrate temporary codes to their permanent locations
item.description: CRD is now mature enough that it makes sense to migrate codes to their permanent locations.  For codes that use the 'code' data type, they'll continue to live in the IG and the change will have no impact on instances.  However, for codes that use CodeableConcept, this will be a breaking change.  To provide continuity, for the next release or two, the IG will expect both the old and new codings to be conveyed.  This will ensure that newer systems will continue to work with old ones.  Eventually, the temporary code system will be deprecated.
item.summary: Migrate temporary codes to their permanent locations

item.title: [FHIR-55266] Potentially re-useable THO-candidate value sets in resource NutritionIntake
item.description:
The NutritionIntake references several value sets that are potential candidates for migration to THO. While value set migration is not required, if it's realistic for the value set definition to need to change in the coming years, or the value sets might be useful in CDA, V2 or other specs, migration should be considered. Specifics: CodeSystem: nutritionintake-status-reason (NutritionIntakeStatusReason) referenced by ValueSet nutritionintake-status-reason bound to element NutritionIntake.statusReason;

(Comment 230 - imported by: Lloyd McKenzie)
item.summary: Potentially re-useable THO-candidate value sets in resource NutritionIntake

status.description: There is agreement that a change of some sort needs to be made in response to the requested change

comments.comment: If the nutrition-intake-status-reason code could be used by real systems, it needs to be in THO.  If not, the code system needs to be marked as 'experimental'

NutritionIntake.statusReason is an example binding with the valueset pointing to two code systems: nutritionintake-status-reason and SNOMED CT International edition 01-Feb 2025. The nutritionintake-status-reason code system is currently in FHIR and either needs to move to THO or both the valueset and code system flagged experimental.

The SNOMED reference is for one value so it is possible that reference could be removed if this is truly an example binding.

item.title: [FHIR-55268] NutritionProduct.status has no escape valve
item.description:
The element NutritionProduct.status is a mandatory element with a required binding, but appears to have neither an 'unknown' or 'other' concept, which can make the containing element impossible to populate in some cases. Please add 'unknown', 'other', or some equivalent escape valve concept to the value set.

(Comment 232 - imported by: Lloyd McKenzie)
item.summary: NutritionProduct.status has no escape valve

status.description: There is agreement that a change of some sort needs to be made in response to the requested change

comments.comment: Thanks - I completely misread that. The text I am referencing about Unknown does not represent "other" is actually on the comment and has nothing to do with the modifier. I agree that this needs an escape valve, at least unknown.
modifier is due to inactive and entered-in-error.

NutritionProduct.status has a modifier stating: "Unknown does not represent "other" - one of the defined statuses must apply. Unknown is used when the authoring system is not sure what the current status is."

This modifier appears to be a holdover from previous iterations of the resource as there is no unknown value present in the current FHIR code system.

item.description: Specify Obligations to identify the functional expectations for the elements.
item.summary: Add Obligations to the profiles

status.description: There is agreement that a change of some sort needs to be made in response to the requested change

comments.comment:
Producer obligations added:

  • MedicationRequest
  • MedicationDispense
  • Medication
  • Dosage

Consumer obligations available in Xt-EHR IG for MedicationRequest and MedicationDispense, but not for the Medication and Dosage. 

Ambiguous mapping between model and profile obligations regarding EHDSMedicationPrescription.dosageInstructions (1..1 SHALL - EHDSDosage). 
EHDSDosage includes:

  • renderedDosageInformation (0..1 SHOULD; maps to MedicationRequest.renderedDosageInstruction) and
  • detailedDosage (0..* no obligation; maps to MedicationRequest.dosageInstruction (Dosage data type).

 

See Xt-EHR Obligations: https://build.fhir.org/ig/Xt-EHR/xt-ehr-common/artifacts.html#6

MPD obligations in profiles: https://build.fhir.org/ig/hl7-eu/mpd-r5/artifacts.html
Meeting minutes: https://confluence.hl7.org/spaces/HEU/pages/413048417/2026-01-22+MPD

item.title: [FHIR-55021] Consider removing FamilyMemberHistory sex element and recommending use of Gender Harmony RSG and/or SPCU FHIR extensions
item.description: The definition of the FamilyMemberHistory resource's sex element is highly non-specific. It is therefore suggested that it be removed in favour of using the Gender Harmony specification's Recorded Sex or Gender (RSG) or Sex Parameter for Clinical Use (SPCU) FHIR extensions.
item.summary: Consider removing FamilyMemberHistory sex element and recommending use of Gender Harmony RSG and/or SPCU FHIR extensions

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

comments.comment: Scheduled for In Person discussion on April 9, 2026 at 5pm Eastern

Sorry having missed the discussion on this but FHIR-17564 does not reflect current understanding and definitions of gender versus sex. Nothing that I see at FHIR-17564 is useful for our current approach.

WGM PCWG meeting with Vocab (Tuesday Q4)

Discussion and vote

  • clarification of the Jira provided. 
  • Description commentary text on the element is not clear
  • Agreement with the gender harmony proposal 
  • suggestion the broader community implementation input is needed
  • The resource maturity level is 2 See https://hl7.org/fhir/R5/versions.html#maturity
  • Motion made to seek input from implementer community for the following: (1) remove the sex element (2) add informative text to recommend both valuesets from the following extensions - https://hl7.org/fhir/extensions/StructureDefinition-individual-recordedSexOrGender.html and https://hl7.org/fhir/extensions/StructureDefinition-patient-sexParameterForClinicalUse.html  
    • Carmela Second
    • Vote: 2 abstain 0 against 24 For
    • Reubin will post on Zulip and contact implementers (Stephen will provide list). Will also send notice via HL7 email.
FYI - Comment was added as part of FHIR-17564

item.title: [FHIR-28585] Date Equality Rules in the Reference Implementation don't match Date Equality rules in FHIRPath, and the RI is more close to what I'd expect
item.description:
Equals in FHIRPath takes into account precision in ways that I wouldn't expect, nor would apparently Grahame according to the RI, per the following comment:

// Per FHIRPath rules, we compare equivalence at the lowest precision of the two values,
// so if we need to, we'll clone either side and reduce its precision

I believe that clarity is needed:

If I say X = Y, the comparison should proceed to the precision given in both.

If precision is UNSPECIFIED in dateTime in X or Y, but not in the other, the comparison should proceed based on Server interpretion of time, as search is performed in FHIR.

This may instead be applicable to https://www.hl7.org/fhir/fhirpath.html#changes
item.summary: Date Equality Rules in the Reference Implementation don't match Date Equality rules in FHIRPath, and the RI is more close to what I'd expect

status.description: Decision has been reached that no change will be made to the specification based on the requested change

comments.comment:
Are you able to please provide more information on this issue so we can resolve or close it?

Thanks.
And don't know how you can have an unspecified precision.

We're not sure we understand what's being asked. Can you provide a link to the FHIR search specification that describes using "Server interpretation of time" to resolve comparisons involving partial date/times?

item.title: [FHIR-51090] Define default-system-version and deprecate system-version
item.description:
The ValueSet/$expand and ValueSet/$validate-code operations have a system-version parameter for specifying the version of a code system used in the operation. They also both have a default-valueset-version parameter, which serves the same purpose but for value sets used in the operation. The default-valueset-version naming is a clearer indication of what is happening, so we propose to:

Add default-system-version, identical to system-version, and
Deprecate system-version

in both operations.
item.summary: Define default-system-version and deprecate system-version

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

comments.comment: I agree, this hybrid wording is better

We have pre-adopted support for all the variations in our R4 implementation.
As a general principle, we preference the more modern variants, but also require that if multiple "equivalent" (renamed) parameters are supplied that they have the same value - there is no "overriding" if the parameters are semantically equivalent.

I would prefer this hybrid rewording:

Specifies a default code system version to use for the operation, IF the value set definition itself does not specify which code system version to use. The format is the same as a canonical URL: [system]|[version] - e.g. http://loinc.org|2.56.
Note that this is a different parameter to systemVersion.

systemVersion is the version of the CodeSystem referenced in the system (IN) parameter, as opposed to any of the CodeSystems referenced in the ValueSet itself (themselves, in the case of included ValueSets).

Ontoserver uses it as a default system version fallback if an explicit default system-version has not been specified.

Discussed on the 2025-12-15 tracker call. 

The request is to rename system-version by going through the mechanics of deprecating system-version and adding default-system-version. 

Discussion: concern about two ways to declare the code system. Leaning towards rejecting this proposal. 

There are two parameters systemVersion and system-version. Agree these are similarly named. 

mjlawley Would like your opinion on whether this change would cause any issues? Attendees are concerned that this change (deprecating and adding) could cause issues. Does your server support all the different input parameters declaring versions of either code systems or value sets? Have you figured out which one overrides the other?

Attendees sense that the description of system-version could be updated to include the word "default" rather than create the deprecated/active scenario. 

Current description:  Specifies a version to use for a system, if the value set does not specify which one to use. The format is the same as a canonical URL: [system]|[version] - e.g. http://loinc.org|2.56. Note that this is a different parameter to systemVersion

Change to:

Specifies a default code system version to use for the operation, IF the value set definition does not specify a code system version. The format is the same as a canonical URL: [system]|[version] - e.g. http://loinc.org|2.56.

This parameter is different from systemVersion. 

(note: the attendees feel the description for systemVersion could be improved however its not clear how its used or what it is intended to be) 

This ticket will be added to the joint TI/FHIR-I session in January 2026.

item.title: [FHIR-55693] Clarify scope of hyperlinks to contained Binary resources
item.description:
(Related to https://jira.hl7.org/browse/FHIR-55401)

So I believe this to be a technical correction in scope rather than a change request – in that it is proposing to make explicit something implicit in the current wording; if folks disagree with me, and think it's a substantive change to what was voted on, of course I am happy to re-submit as a change request at the appropriate time.

Propose to add a clarifying statement of intent to:
"It should be noted that hyperlinks in XHTML or supportLink may point to 'contained' binary resources. Such resources SHALL either be PDFs or XHTML pages that adhere to FHIR's 'safe' HTML rules (no active content or scripts)"
so that it reads
"It should be noted that hyperlinks in XHTML or supportLink may point to 'contained' binary resources. Such resources SHALL either be PDFs or XHTML pages that adhere to FHIR's 'safe' HTML rules (no active content or scripts). *Hyperlinks in these XHTML pages may themselves point to 'contained' binary resources.*"
item.summary: Clarify scope of hyperlinks to contained Binary resources

Reopening a ballot requires adhering to HL7 rules around revisiting decisions. (Advance notice and 2/3 majority vote in favor of revisiting is generally required.) Include a reference to the minutes of the decision to revisit in the comments as well as rationale for re-opening the item.
This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Will include the text requested in the description:

"Hyperlinks in these XHTML pages may themselves point to 'contained' binary resources."

We will add the following guidance: "Such contained Binary pages SHALL NOT point to other contained resources unless those resources are pointed to directly by other elements in the Questionnaire.  Implementers MAY embed images or other content using the 'data' mechanism, or may opt to combine multiple files into a single instance to eliminate the need for inter-linking."

Treating this as a non-substantive change even though it's adding a SHALL NOT because the statement is merely reflecting technical reality - there's no conformant mechanism that allows a contained Binary to point to another contained Binary.

item.title: [FHIR-55612] Add curationCoverage extension
item.description:
Data analytics use cases, especially in public health incident reporting, rely on curated value sets as part of the criteria for establishing reportable events. Due to the number and size of these value sets, as well as practical resource limitations involved in their maintenance, it is not always possible to ensure 100% curation of the content, and when this is the case, it is useful to communicate the relative curation coverage of the value sets involved.

To support this case, the following extension is proposed:

crmi-curationCoverage, 0..1 complex Relative curation level of the codes in the value set

  • status 1..1 CodeableConcept bound extensible to CurationCoverageStatus
  • statusReason 0..1 markdown
  • author 0..1 string
  • dateTime 0..1 dateTime
  • note 0..1 markdown

In addition, define an invariant to ensure:
INV: status ~ ‘partial’ implies statusReason.exists()

Add this as an extension, 0..1 in the CRMIPublishableValueSet profile.

CurationCoverageStatusCodes

  • complete -
  • partial -

Definitions forthcoming.
item.summary: Add curationCoverage extension

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: https://github.com/HL7/crmi-ig/pull/97

Proposed CurationCoverageStatusCodes code definitions:

‘complete’ - All codes considered relevant to the intended clinical scope of this value set have been proactively identified, reviewed, and curated by the steward. No known gaps or missing component groupings remain.

Short display - Complete clinical coverage

‘partial’ - The value set has not yet been fully curated for the intended clinical scope. Relevant codes or component groupings may be missing, unreviewed, or not yet incorporated, including cases where expected referenced value sets have not yet been included.

Short display - Incomplete clinical coverage

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Define an IG specific extension:

level 1..1 CodeableConcept    (bound extensible to a value set with all concept codes in CurationCoverageLevel)
levelReason 0..1 markdown
author 0..1 string
dateTime 0..1 dateTime
note 0..1 markdown

In addition, define an invariant to ensure:

Add this as an extension, 0..1 in the CRMIPublishableValueSet profile.

Define CurationCoverageLevelCodes  (in the CRMI IG):  

http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/curation-coverge-level-codes 

‘complete’ - All concept codes considered relevant to the intended scope of this value set have been proactively identified, reviewed, and curated by the steward. No known gaps or missing component groupings remain.

Short display - Complete coverage

‘partial’ - The value set has not yet been fully curated for the intended scope. Relevant concept codes or component groupings may be missing, or not yet incorporated, including cases where expected referenced value sets have not yet been included.

Short display - Partial coverage

item.title: [FHIR-55866] Clarify where the CQL Identifier mime type gets used
item.description:
The first paragraph of Use of CQL section states "The mime type that SHOULD be used for a CQL Identifier is "text/cql-identifier". §spec-86"

This sentence is missing context, specifically where the mime type for a CQL Identifier gets used. Provide a link of other detail so that readers know where and how the mime type gets used.

item.title: [FHIR-55883] Clarify §spec-125
item.description:
At the end of the Retrieving Questionnaire Packages section is a note containing requirement spec-125 that states

Previously completed QuestionnaireResponses SHALL NOT be supported due to concerns about currency of clinical information. §spec-125

What this means and which actor it pertains to (the requirement is mapped to the DTR Client) is unclear in the context of the section. Is the intention that

  1. DTR Clients should not use a QuestionnaireResponse completed earlier to complete a newly-received Questionnaire, or
  2. DTR Servers should not accept the submission of QuestionnaireResponses they've previously received, or
  3. something else?

Having written them out, option 1 could make sense in this context. If that is right, clarify what "be supported" means in the requirement text, such as this:

"Previously completed QuestionnaireResponses SHALL NOT be used by DTR clients to complete Questionnaires received due to concerns about currency of clinical information. §spec-125
item.summary: Clarify §spec-125

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

item.title: [FHIR-55884] Requirements spec-47 and spec-155 in conflict
item.description:
Requirements around primary keys and update behavior for coverage-information extensions are out of sync between server and client sections:

  • For servers, In the 3rd to last paragraph of the Determinations from Adaptive Forms section, it says "For the purpose of determining whether to update or replace, the combination of the 'coverage' reference and the coverage-assertion-id SHALL be treated as a primary key (i.e., if the coverage and coverage-assertion-id is the same, then the expectation is to replace the existing coverage-information repetition. If either are different then the intention is to add an additional coverage-information extension repetition to the list.) §spec-47"
  • For clients, In the Passing DTR QuestionnaireResponses to Downstream Processes section, it says "If a coverage-information already exists on the target resource with the same coverage-information.coverage, it SHALL be overridden. §spec-155"

The primary key needs to be the same in both cases so that clients perform the actions that servers expect.
item.summary: Requirements spec-47 and spec-155 in conflict

item.title: [FHIR-55885] Clarify responsibility for requirement spec-159
item.description:
The last paragraph of the Passing DTR QuestionnaireResponses to Downstream Processes section states: "If there is a desire to send different content to different recipients, then distinct QuestionnaireResponses SHALL be used. §spec-159 "

Clarify the mechanism by which "distinct QuestionnaireResponses" are created. Is this

  • The responsibility of the DTR client to duplicate the QuestionnaireResponse
  • The responsibility of the DTR client to make DTR requests and complete forms for each of the intended recipients.
  • The responsibility of the DTR Server / payer to provide separate Questionnaire / QuestionnaireResponse instances for each recipient.
  • Something else?

The requirement is currently mapped to the DTR Client actor.
item.summary: Clarify responsibility for requirement spec-159

Will update to make clear that the responsibility rests with the DTR server to produce distinct questionnaires.  Specifically, will reword from:

"If there is a desire to send different content to different recipients, then distinct QuestionnaireResponses SHALL be used"

to

"If the initial QuestionnaireResponse has multiple purposes (e.g. for prior auth, claim submission, and performer transmission), the DTR server organization SHALL ensure that the full completed QuestionnaireResponse package is appropriate for all uses.  If there is a need to send different information to different recipients for different purposes, the DTR server organization SHALL produce distinct Questionnaires such that QuestionnaireResponses only contain information that can be shared without redaction for their listed purposes."

Marking this as non-substantive as it's not changing the requirement, merely making it more clear.

item.title: [FHIR-55910] intermixed clinical and admin questions
item.description: It seems as though the spec allows clinical and administrative questions to be mixed without workflow safeguards. In order to meet the needs of burden reduction would suggest tightening this, for example adding something like: DTR services SHALL ensure clinical questions can be completed independently of administrative questions when both are required. Administrative questions SHALL NOT block completion of clinically relevant documentation unless required by coverage policy.
item.summary: intermixed clinical and admin questions

status.description: Decision has been reached that no change will be made to the specification based on the requested change

comments.comment: https://confluence.hl7.org/spaces/CDS/pages/413255425/2026-02-25+-+Meeting+Agenda
Fine with disposition. Current texts is sufficient
Reviewed and accepted proposed disposition on Feb. 20 BR call

Please indicate why this issue is no longer considered to be a duplicate.

Re-open Issue:

Re-opening an issue will cause any associated withdrawn negatives associated with issue and any duplicates of this issue to be re-instated

Reopening a ballot requires adhering to HL7 rules around revisiting decisions. (Advance notice and 2/3 majority vote in favor of revisiting is generally required.) Include a reference to the minutes of the decision to revisit in the comments as well as rationale for re-opening the item.
This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: We already have language [here](https://build.fhir.org/ig/HL7/davinci-dtr/en/specification.html#ci-c-spec-22) that sets expectations that clinical information be front-loaded as much as possible.  However, there will be situations where the determination of what clinical questions need to be asked will be dependent on administrative information.  For example, administrative staff could easily answer "has the patient received this medication in the previous 5 years" - it doesn't necessarily require clinical expertise.  However, the answer to that question might trigger additional questions that do require clinical expertise and/or collection of data from the patient.  It doesn't make sense to say "you can't have decision logic that depends on administrative questions" because the alternative then would be to ask all of the potentially needed clinical questions, even if 80% of the answers won't be needed.  This would be unhelpful in protecting provider time.

item.title: [FHIR-55692] Clarification of location of 'contained' Binary XHTML/PDF/image resources
item.description:
Since:

  1. This resolution of Binary resources should work both for standard and adaptive flows – where the Questionnaire resource to be rendered in the client is itself a `contained` resource of the QuestionnaireResponse, and
  2. Contained resources cannot themselves have contained resources (https://hl7.org/fhir/R4/references.html#contained)

It may be worth clarifying for the adaptive case that the referenced resources may be contained in the root resource (QuestionnaireResponse). I don't think this is called out in the base docs https://hl7.org/fhir/R4/narrative.html#id because, also, contained resources shouldn't have `narrative`, but `rendering-xhtml` in a Questionnaire is not narrative even though it is subject to the same limitations.
item.summary: Clarification of location of 'contained' Binary XHTML/PDF/image resources

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: https://confluence.hl7.org/spaces/CDS/pages/413255425/2026-02-25+-+Meeting+Agenda
Typo.  That should be '2/19/2026'
Reviewed and approved on BR call 2/9/2026
Yep!
Update after internal discussion: We can work with the fallback, that's a feasible solution.
Just to me it seems likely to be workable, but I need to talk with some SMEs about it on Monday to give a definitive answer.
Some implementers might not validate, but if they don't there are a wide variety of things they'll get wrong.  What are your thoughts about the fallback for the other issue?
If you think that'll be clear to implementers without extra verbiage in the DTR guide, then this seems like this can probably just be closed.

Perhaps it is relevant to the other one.  The question of where the contained resources are allowed to go is clear.  There's a constraint that says contained resources can't have contained resources, so there's only one place they can go, which is inside the QuestionnaireResponse.  Anything else would fail validation.

https://hl7.org/fhir/R4/references.html#contained says

  • Contained resources share the same internal id resolution space as the parent resource (for id attributes, see Narrative references).
  • When resolving references, references are resolved by looking through the 'container' resource - the one that contains the other resources. Since there are no nested contained resources, there is only one container resource.

Which suggests that this clarification isn't strictly necessary, but that documenting the implied behaviour might make for more clarity in the DTR spec.

lloyd I'm not quite sure I'm following the question; isn't it more germane to https://jira.hl7.org/browse/FHIR-55693?

The main case I want to make sure is documented here is where:

  1. The Questionnaire is adaptive, so that what gets rendered by the client is the Questionnaire contained in the QuestionnaireResponse returned by $questionnaire-package
  2. There are support{Hyper}Links in the questionnaire of the form '#id' that reference contained binaries
  3. The Questionnaire, being contained, can't itself contain the binaries

And it seemed like the most parsimonious/idiomatic solution is that the DTR client should look in QuestionnaireResponse.contained, for the qnr's sibling contained resources, to find the XHTML doc/PDF (or image)

Fallback alternative if we don't get a viable solution:

Binaries can't point to other binaries.  Instead, embed images inside the link using 'data', and combine all web-pages into a single page, with links jumping to the appropriate section.  Not ideal, but presumably workable.  benet.devereux@optum.com, would that work for you for now if that turns out to be the best we can do?
See https://chat.fhir.org/#narrow/channel/179166-implementers/topic/Contained.20Binary.20pointing.20to.20contained.20Binary/with/573803219
Related to the change request https://jira.hl7.org/browse/FHIR-55401
